commentary:
# Exchange Liquidity Commentary – 02 Jul

NYSE dominates liquidity rankings with an exceptional ILLIQ of 0.0019, substantially outperforming peers and reflecting its unmatched trading volume and tight spreads across all metrics. Conversely, LSE ranks sixth with ILLIQ of 0.1366—roughly 72 times higher—alongside the widest AR spread at 0.8128%. Nasdaq 100 presents an intriguing anomaly: despite competitive ILLIQ of 0.0033, its Roll spread of 1.7361% significantly exceeds peers, suggesting potential intraday volatility or inventory effects. European exchanges (Euronext, SIX, Xetra) cluster in the middle band with moderate but elevated illiquidity metrics.
